1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
239

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
11	
12	/-!
13	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
14	
15	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
17	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
18	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
19	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
20	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
21	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
22	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
23	
24	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
26	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
27	
28	The companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (rank =
29	relative dimension `n` from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`) is
30	deferred to iter-132+ for blueprint-RHS-pinning work; not in this file.
31	
32	## Status (iter-131 fix-up: pure-term body refactor)
33	
34	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
35	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
36	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
37	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
38	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
39	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
40	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
41	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
42	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
43	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
44	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
45	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
46	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
47	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
48	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
49	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
50	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
51	the outer expression is the explicit
52	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
53	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
54	binder), is preserved. The companion rank lemma
55	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` lives in a follow-up declaration
56	(not in this file); the iter-131 body shape is the testable deliverable
57	witnessed by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
58	
59	## References
60	
61	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
62	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
63	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
64	- Project infrastructure consumed:
65	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
66	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
67	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
68	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
69	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
70	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
71	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
72	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
73	-/
74	
75	set_option autoImplicit false
76	
77	universe u
78	
79	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
80	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
81	
82	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
83	
84	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
85	
86	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
87	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
88	`ModuleCat k`).
89	
90	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
91	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
92	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
93	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
94	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
95	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
96	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
97	iter-132+ companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (in
98	a follow-up declaration) pins the rank to `n` from the
99	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
100	
101	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
102	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
103	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
104	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
105	
106	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
107	change).** The body is built as follows:
108	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
109	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
110	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
111	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
112	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
113	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
114	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
115	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
116	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
117	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
118	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
119	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
120	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
121	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
122	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
123	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
124	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
125	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
126	   `k`-module.
127	
128	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
129	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
130	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
131	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
132	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
133	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
134	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
135	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
136	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
137	the iter-132+ rank lemma can rewrite against. For the live consumer
138	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
139	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
140	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
141	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
142	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
143	
144	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The structural
145	properties (`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`, rank `= n`) are content
146	for the iter-132+ rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`; the
147	structural-shape accessibility needed by that lemma is witnessed here by
148	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`. -/
149	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
150	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
151	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
152	    ModuleCat k :=
153	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
154	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
155	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
156	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
157	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
158	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
159	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
160	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
161	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
162	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
163	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
164	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
165	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
166	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
167	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
168	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
169	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
170	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
171	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
172	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
173	    exact hxV
174	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
175	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
176	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
177	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
178	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
179	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
180	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
181	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
182	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
183	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
184	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
185	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
186	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
187	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
188	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
189	
190	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
191	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra

\begin{proof}

\leanok
  The identity section $\eta_G$ is a canonical $k$-rational point of $G$ supplied by the $\GrpObj$ structure. The iter-130 Lean construction realises $\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ as a chart-base-changed Kähler module: by the forward Jacobian criterion \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (already in the tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}), there exists an affine chart $V \subseteq G$ around the image of the identity section $\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})$, with affine base chart $U \subseteq \Spec k$ and the structural ring map $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(G, V)$ exhibiting \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} $n$ (and consequently $\Omega_{\Gamma(G,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(G, V)$-module of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). The cotangent space at the identity is then defined as the base change to $k$ of this algebraic Kähler module along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained by restricting the identity section $\eta_G$ to $V$ and composing with the canonical isomorphism $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$:
  \[
    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;\;:=\;\; k \,\otimes_{\Gamma(G,\,V)}\, \Omega_{\Gamma(G,\,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}.
  \]
  This is a finitely generated free $k$-module of rank $n$ by base-change of a free module along $\psi_V$ (the rank is preserved; see \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}).

  \emph{Caveat on canonicity.} The construction depends on a chart choice $V$ extracted via \texttt{Classical.choice} from the existential statement of \texttt{smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; it is therefore not canonically attached to $G$ in the strictest sense. For the live consumer (rigidity over $k$, via \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}), the existence of a rank-$n$ free $k$-module is the only structural content needed, so canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent) presentation via the stalk-side cotangent $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ is available as a future bridge (\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} below, currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}) at additional cost; the project defers that bridge until a non-rigidity consumer materialises that requires canonicity.
\end{proof}

\emph{Step 1: from the chart-base-changed Kähler module to the differential module of the local stalk-residue map.} The iter-131 body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} is the chart-base-change

To bridge this chart-base-changed module to the stalk-side cotangent, factor $\psi_V$ through the localisation at the identity stalk (and a parallel base-side localisation $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to k$):
  \[
    \Gamma(G, V) \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{loc}\;}\; \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} \;\xrightarrow{\;\pi\;}\; k,
    \qquad
    \Gamma(\Spec k, U) \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{loc}\;}\; k.

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}
  Combine four standard pieces.

  \emph{Step 1: bridge to the local-ring cotangent space.} By \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}, the iter-128 evaluate-then-extend-scalars body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} is $k$-linearly isomorphic to $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G} / \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$, the local-ring cotangent space of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$. The Mathlib container for the RHS is \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (defined as the abbreviation \texttt{(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}); rank is preserved across the bridge isomorphism, so it suffices to compute $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = n$.

  \emph{Step 2: regularity of the local ring at the identity.} Smoothness of $f \colon G \to \Spec k$ at the closed point $\eta_G$ implies that $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ is a regular local ring (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}; Stacks Tag~00TR / Hartshorne~II.10.3.B). The Krull dimension of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ equals the relative dimension $n$ of $f$ at $\eta_G$: this is the standard smooth-implies-rel-dim-equals-Krull-dim-of-the-stalk fact (Stacks Tag~02G1 / Hartshorne~II.10.4 for the smooth case). Both facts package together in the Mathlib \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ class via the local-ring instance attached to a closed point of a smooth scheme.

  \emph{Step 3: cotangent rank equals Krull dimension for a regular local ring.} For a regular local ring $(R, \mathfrak m)$, the cotangent space \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace}~$R$ is finitely generated free over the residue field with rank equal to the Krull dimension; this is essentially the defining property of regularity (and is precisely the equivalence between Noetherian-local minimal-number-of-generators and Krull-dim that \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} packages). Combined with Step 2: $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = \dim \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} = n$.

  \emph{Step 4: cross-check via the affine-chart Kähler rank.} An alternative route through the project's already-formalised forward direction of the smoothness criterion (\cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}, i.e.\ \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}) reaches the same rank: on a standard-smooth affine chart $(U, V)$ around $\eta_G$, the algebra-Kähler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}); extension of scalars along the residue map at $\eta_G$ preserves the rank to give $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} = n$. Either route closes the rank lemma; the bridge-via-regular-local-ring route is preferred because it avoids the affine-chart machinery and is more directly stated in the Mathlib \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} idiom that the iter-128 body construction naturally lands in.

  Finally, $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g = \finrank_{k}\,\mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k) = \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} = n$ by the standard rank-equals-rank-of-dual identity for finitely-generated free modules over a field.

  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The closure pieces are \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (the local-ring cotangent module; \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}), \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} (Step 2's regularity output; \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs}), the Krull-dimension-equals-cotangent-rank fact attached to \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}, and (for the cross-check route) \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) together with \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} of Differentials.tex.

  \emph{Iter-130 closure path under Replacement (B).} The body of \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} (post-iter-130 body swap) is \texttt{(ModuleCat.extendScalars $\psi_V$.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of $\Gamma(G, V)$ $\Omega[\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)]$)} for a chart $V$ around $\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})$. \begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge, thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
  We close the rank computation under Replacement (B) (the iter-131 chart-base-change body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}) using the existential output of \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} directly. The proof is in four steps: Steps 1+2 form the live closure path; Step 3 documents an alternative canonical route through $\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$ that is currently deferred under Replacement (B); Step 4 carries the conclusion across $k$-linear duality to the tangent side.

  \emph{Step 1: chart-side Kähler rank.} Let $V \subseteq G.\mathrm{left}$ and $U \subseteq \Spec k$ be the affine charts (around the image $x_0 = \eta_G(\mathrm{pt}) \in G.\mathrm{left}$) extracted by \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (project lemma \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}); the same charts are pinned by the body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} via the iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain and re-extractable through the companion lemma \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars} (see \S "Iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain body shape" below for the recommended downstream rewrite pattern). The existential's payload, on the $\Gamma(\Spec k, U)$-algebra $\Gamma(G, V)$ (with the algebra structure given by the appLE ring map of $G.\mathrm{hom}$), supplies in one shot both
  \[
    \texttt{hfree} \colon \;\texttt{Module.Free}\;\Gamma(G, V)\;\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)\,/\,\Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}
    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
    \texttt{hrank} \colon \;\texttt{Module.rank}\;\Gamma(G, V)\;\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)\,/\,\Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)} \;=\; n,
  \]
  internally consuming the Mathlib lemmas \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). Converting the rank-as-cardinal equation \texttt{hrank} to a $\finrank$ equation via \texttt{Module.finrank\_eq\_of\_rank\_eq} (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Finrank}; for a commutative semiring $R$ with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}, $\texttt{Module.rank}\,R\,M = \uparrow n \Rightarrow \texttt{Module.finrank}\,R\,M = n$) gives
  \[
    \finrank_{\Gamma(G, V)}\,\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)\,/\,\Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)} \;=\; n.
  \]

  \emph{Step 2: base-change to $k$ preserves $\finrank$.} By construction, the body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} is the base change $k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}$ along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained from the identity section restricted to $V$ (composed with $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$). The Mathlib base-change lemma
  \[
    \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange} \colon\;\;
    \texttt{Module.finrank}\;R\;(\texttt{TensorProduct}\;S\;R\;M')
      \;=\; \texttt{Module.finrank}\;S\;M'
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Constructions}; requires $S$ a commutative semiring with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}, $M'$ a \texttt{Module.Free}\;$S$-module, and $R$ an $S$-algebra with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}), applied with $S := \Gamma(G, V)$, $R := k$, and $M' := \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}$ — using \texttt{hfree} from Step 1 to discharge the \texttt{Module.Free} hypothesis — yields
  \[
    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; \finrank_{k}\bigl(k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}\bigr) \;=\; \finrank_{\Gamma(G, V)}\,\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)} \;=\; n,
  \]
  which closes the rank lemma on the live (B) path.

  \emph{Companion freeness fact (downstream consumer; not on the rank-lemma critical path).} For downstream consumers that separately need $k$-freeness of $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ — notably \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free} of piece (i.c) — the typeclass \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.instFree} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Free}) supplies $\texttt{Module.Free}\;k\;(k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)})$ directly from \texttt{hfree}. This is informational here; the rank lemma above closes without invoking it.

  \emph{Step 3 (alternative canonical route, currently deferred).} An alternative closure via the stalk-side cotangent space $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ is available in principle and was the iter-128-era plan. The route is: (i) bridge from the chart-base-changed body to the local-stalk Kähler module via \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} (currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}; identifies $k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)} \cong \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$, via a localisation-at-the-identity-stalk step composed with the standard $k \otimes_{R} \Omega_{R/k} \cong \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$ identification — Stacks Tag~02G1 / Hartshorne~II.8.7); (ii) invoke regularity of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}; from smoothness of $G \to \Spec k$ at the closed point $\eta_G$, Stacks Tag~00TR / Hartshorne~II.10.3.B), with Krull dimension of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ equal to the relative dimension $n$ (Stacks Tag~02G1); (iii) read off the rank from the defining property of regular local rings, $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = \dim \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} = n$, packaged into \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}) and \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs}). This route is preferred only when a downstream consumer requires the canonical (chart-independent) stalk-side presentation; it is \emph{not} on the live closure path under Replacement (B) because the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} is \texttt{\textbackslash notready}. The footer paragraph below records the iter-131 strategy-critic verdict on this deferral.

  \emph{Step 4: dualisation conclusion ($\finrank\,\mathfrak g = n$).} By the standard rank-equals-rank-of-dual identity for finitely-generated free modules over a field,
  \[
    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g \;=\; \finrank_{k}\,\mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k) \;=\; \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; n,
  \]
  delivering the consequent $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g = n$ on the tangent side.

  \emph{Mathlib name summary (live closure path under Replacement (B)).} The closure pieces consumed by Steps 1+2 above are: \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (project lemma \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; supplies the existential $\exists\,U\,V\,e\,(h_{xV} : x_0 \in V)\,(h_U\,h_V : \texttt{IsAffineOpen}\;\cdot)\,(\texttt{hfree})\,(\texttt{hrank})$ in one shot, internally consuming \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential}), \texttt{Module.finrank\_eq\_of\_rank\_eq} (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Finrank}; rank-as-cardinal to $\finrank$ converter), and \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange} (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Constructions}; base-change preserves $\finrank$ when the source module is \texttt{Module.Free}). The companion freeness typeclass \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.instFree} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Free}) is informational for downstream consumers (e.g.\ \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free}) but does not enter the rank-lemma proof. All four names are verified in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} by the iter-132 plan agent.

  \emph{Iter-131 strategy-critic Q4 collapse (footer).} The iter-131 strategy-critic Q4 review of \cref{subsec:RigidityKbar_piece_i_decomposition} documented in STRATEGY.md that, under Replacement (B), the trio (definition + bridge + rank) collapses to a duo (definition + rank): the rank lemma closes directly from \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}'s existential without routing through the bridge \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}. The bridge therefore becomes vestigial on the live path and is currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}; it re-enters the live closure only if trigger (a') at iter-133+ piece (i.b) fires — i.e., only if a non-rigidity consumer materialises that requires the canonical (chart-independent) stalk-side cotangent presentation $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ that the bridge supplies.
\end{proof}

114	\leanok
115	  The identity section $\eta_G$ is a canonical $k$-rational point of $G$ supplied by the $\GrpObj$ structure. The iter-131 Lean construction (Replacement (B), pure-term \texttt{noncomputable def}) realises $\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ as a chart-base-changed Kähler module: by the forward Jacobian criterion \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (already in the tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}), there exists an affine chart $V \subseteq G$ around the image of the identity section $\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})$, with affine base chart $U \subseteq \Spec k$ and the structural ring map $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(G, V)$ exhibiting \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} $n$ (and consequently $\Omega_{\Gamma(G,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(G, V)$-module of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). The cotangent space at the identity is then defined as the base change to $k$ of this algebraic Kähler module along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained by restricting the identity section $\eta_G$ to $V$ and composing with the canonical isomorphism $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$:
116	  \[
117	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;\;:=\;\; k \,\otimes_{\Gamma(G,\,V)}\, \Omega_{\Gamma(G,\,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}.
118	  \]
119	  This is a finitely generated free $k$-module of rank $n$ by base-change of a free module along $\psi_V$ (the rank is preserved; see \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}).
120	
121	  \emph{Caveat on canonicity.} The construction depends on a chart choice $V$ extracted via a \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain from the existential statement of \texttt{smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; it is therefore not canonically attached to $G$ in the strictest sense. (The iter-131 fix-up refactored what was previously a top-level \texttt{Classical.choice}-wrapper into a pure-term \texttt{let}-chain of \texttt{Classical.choose} / \texttt{Classical.choose\_spec} accessors, exposing the outer head symbol \texttt{(ModuleCat.extendScalars\,$\psi_V$.hom).obj\,(ModuleCat.of\,$\Gamma$(G, V)\,$\Omega$[$\Gamma$(G, V)\,/\,$\Gamma$(Spec k, U)])} to downstream consumers; see \S "Iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain body shape" below for the Lean encoding details, and the companion lemma \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars} for the structural-shape rewrite handle.) For the live consumer (rigidity over $k$, via \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}), the existence of a rank-$n$ free $k$-module is the only structural content needed, so canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent) presentation via the stalk-side cotangent $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ is available as a future bridge (\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} below, currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}) at additional cost; the project defers that bridge until a non-rigidity consumer materialises that requires canonicity.
